metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that an impact-labeled 'block-worthy vs nit' adjudicated dataset exists (or explicitly scope its creation as in-plan work) so the A/B harness in AC4 has a ground-truth corpus to evaluate separation against.
findings.finding: The plan's AC4 and What section assert that the A/B harness will 'score the labeled/adjudicated set' and 'measure separation of block-worthy vs nit on the adjudicated set', implying such an impact-labeled adjudicated dataset already exists. No such dataset is found in the codebase: the plan-review adjudicated gold set (eval_specs/plan-review-finder.eval.yaml) labels criterion-level pass/fail verdicts, not impact scores; no code-review impact-labeled adjudicated set is found anywhere. The assertion that a 'block-worthy vs nit' labeled set is available to gate threshold changes is unverifiable from the codebase.
findings.location: What section ('scores the labeled/adjudicated set with the new model') and AC4 ('gates any threshold-down tuning on beating the current model')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a Scope bullet explicitly creating a minimal labeled corpus (e.g., N≥30 findings hand-labeled block-worthy/nit from the existing REVIEW_RESULT sidecar corpus) as a deliverable of this ticket or a named dependency, so AC4's gating condition is concrete and satisfiable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify or create the impact-labeled adjudicated set the A/B harness will score against (file path, format, minimum size) and add it to Scope, or explicitly state that assembling it is a prerequisite task gating AC4.
findings.finding: AC4 refers to gating 'any threshold-down tuning on beating the current model' using 'the adjudicated set', but no impact-labeled adjudicated dataset for code-review findings exists in the repo as a standalone artifact. The plan-review finder gold_set (`plan-review-finder.eval.yaml`) labels recall/no-fire, not block-worthy vs nit separation on impact scores.
findings.location: AC4: 'measures separation of block-worthy vs nit on the adjudicated set'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define and locate the 'labeled/adjudicated set' the A/B harness requires (AC4): specify how 'block-worthy vs nit' ground truth is assigned, where the corpus lives, and how large it needs to be before the gate is meaningful.
findings.finding: The A/B harness (AC4) gates any threshold-down change on 'beating the current model' on 'the labeled/adjudicated set', but no such labeled/adjudicated corpus exists anywhere in the codebase or docs — its existence, labeling methodology, and 'block-worthy vs nit' ground-truth definition are entirely unverified.
findings.location: AC4: 'gates any threshold-down tuning on beating the current model'; What section: 'scores the labeled/adjudicated set with the new model'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define (or reference) the labeled/adjudicated set the A/B harness scores against: specify its location, format, how block-worthy vs. nit labels are assigned, and whether it needs to be constructed as part of this work or pre-exists.
findings.finding: AC4 and the What section gate any threshold-down change on the A/B harness 'scoring the labeled/adjudicated set,' but no such labeled/adjudicated corpus is defined, located, or specified in the plan. The repo contains an eval gold_set for the plan-review finder (confirmed: `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-finder.eval.yaml`), but no impact-labeled corpus suitable for measuring block-worthy vs. nit separation exists or is referenced.
findings.location: What section ('scores the labeled/adjudicated set') / AC4

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the canonical `impact_model_version` value vocabulary (e.g., as a named constant or enum in the sidecar module, or in a schema doc), specifying valid values, naming convention, and where the version string is authoritatively declared for each gate.
findings.finding: The `impact_model_version` tag is introduced as a segmentation enum in the sidecar schema and as the key for the 'refuses to pool' calibration-replay guard, but its value vocabulary (e.g., what strings are valid, how versioning is named/incremented) is never defined anywhere in the plan, and the field is absent from both existing `build_payload()` implementations (confirmed: `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py` and `src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py` have no such field).
findings.location: What section / AC2 / src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py build_payload() / src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py build_payload()
findings.suggested_fix: Add a module-level constant in each sidecar (e.g., `IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION = "plan-v2"`) and a schema note listing valid values; reference these constants in `build_payload()` and in the calibration script's filter logic.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete specification for the 'refuses to pool' decision rule to the plan (or directly to the calibration script extension section): define whether it hard-errors on a mixed-version corpus, emits separate per-version tables, or requires an explicit `--model-version` filter flag.
findings.finding: The plan specifies that the calibration replay 'refuses to pool scores across versions,' but this processing protocol is not co-located with the sidecar schema or the calibration script. The existing `calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py` has no version-filtering logic whatsoever (confirmed by read), and the plan gives no algorithmic specification of how 'refusing to pool' is implemented (e.g., hard error on mixed-version corpus? silent per-version segmentation? a flag?).
findings.location: What section / AC2 / docs/experiments/calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least a thin automated check for the AC2 guard (e.g., a unit/integration test that feeds mixed-version sidecar records to the replay and asserts it raises/rejects rather than pooling) and for AC3 sidecar field presence (structural assertion that `impact_model_version`, join keys, and tier-tagged sub-answers appear in emitted sidecars).
findings.finding: The plan introduces a concrete guard — the calibration replay 'refuses to pool scores across versions' — which is new conditional/failure-path logic, yet no testing strategy is stated for this guard or for the `impact_model_version` sidecar field emission (AC2, AC3).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2 ('the calibration replay filters by it and refuses to pool scores across versions'); AC3 ('Both gates emit sidecars carrying the new impact + tier-tagged sub-answers + join keys')
findings.suggested_fix: In the calibrate script, add a pytest parametrize case that builds a fixture corpus with two `impact_model_version` values and asserts the replay raises `VersionMixError` (or equivalent) rather than returning pooled stats. For AC3, add a schema-validation step in the data-capture child's test suite (or here if it ships together) that asserts all required sidecar keys are non-null on a sample review run.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that 'Config flags for permissive thresholds' reuses the existing `blocking_enabled` / `block_threshold` / `default_posture` surface in `criteria_routing.json` (the WS5 pattern) rather than introducing a new config key; if a new surface is genuinely needed, document why the existing mechanism is insufficient.
findings.finding: The plan says 'Config flags for permissive thresholds' without specifying whether the implementation uses the EXISTING `blocking_enabled` / `block_threshold` / `default_posture` mechanism in `criteria_routing.json` (already used for WS5's permissive code-review rollout) or introduces a new toggle surface. If new config keys or rebar.toml flags are added, this is config-surface proliferation on top of two already-established mechanisms for the same concept.
findings.location: Scope section — 'Config flags for permissive thresholds'; AC #1 — 'New impact models ship behind permissive thresholds'
findings.suggested_fix: Set `blocking_enabled: false` (code-review gate) or `default_posture: advisory` + `block_threshold: 0.95` (plan-review gate) on the new impact-model criteria entries in the respective `criteria_routing.json` files — the same mechanism WS5 used — rather than adding a new flag.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify how `material_fingerprint` is computed for the code-review gate sidecar — confirm whether the plan-review `PlanContext`-based function is extended to accept a diff/PR context, or whether a separate code-review-native content hash is defined; document the join semantics.
findings.finding: The plan lists `material_fingerprint` as a join key emitted by *both* gates' sidecars, but the existing `material_fingerprint` implementation (`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/pass1.py:496`, `orchestrator.material_fingerprint`) is defined over a `PlanContext` (hashes plan text + AC fields) — a plan-review-specific concept with no analog in a code-review diff. Applying the same function unchanged to code-review findings would golden-hammer a plan-scoped abstraction onto a diff-scoped domain, and a previous cached review finding confirms this tension.
findings.location: What section — 'Both gates emit sidecars carrying … `material_fingerprint` join keys'; AC #3 — 'Both gates emit sidecars carrying the new impact + tier-tagged sub-answers + join keys'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the field layout for 'tier-tagged sub-answers' in the sidecar schema (or plan body), specifying the key name, value type, and how tier labels map to sub-answer content for both the plan-review and code-review sidecar paths.
findings.finding: The What section and AC3 reference 'tier-tagged sub-answers' as a sidecar content field that both gates must emit, but this field's structure is never defined. The existing plan-review sidecar `_slim()` carries `tier` and `verification` (with `binary` sub-answers), while the code-review sidecar `build_payload()` carries only top-level verdict arrays — neither has a field called 'tier-tagged sub-answers,' and no layout for such a field is given.
findings.location: What section ('Both gates emit sidecars carrying the new impact + tier-tagged sub-answers') / AC3 / src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py _slim() / src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py build_payload()

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define how the calibration replay handles sidecars that predate the `impact_model_version` tag (absent field): specify whether they are treated as a named legacy version, excluded with a warning, or back-filled.
findings.finding: The plan does not address consumer behavior when `impact_model_version` is absent in legacy sidecars (written before this rollout). The replay is specified to 'refuse to pool across versions' but it is unspecified how it handles sidecars that predate the tag entirely.
findings.location: What — 'the calibration replay filters by it and refuses to pool scores across versions'; AC line 2
findings.suggested_fix: Assign absent `impact_model_version` to a sentinel value (e.g., 'pre-v2-unknown') during replay ingestion; log the count; never silently drop or pool them with a named version.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the dual-write window policy: define what the replay does when `impact_model_version` is absent (e.g., treat NULL as sentinel `'plan-v1'`/`'code-v1'` legacy version rather than error), and document this in both the AC and the calibration script contract.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify how the calibration replay handles REVIEW_RESULT sidecars that lack the `impact_model_version` tag — documents written by old-code instances during the rollout window (or all pre-rollout historical sidecars). The plan states the replay 'refuses to pool scores across versions', but if absence of the tag causes a hard rejection or silent exclusion, a large portion of the corpus is lost with no specified handling path.
findings.location: What section / AC line 2: 'the calibration replay filters by it and refuses to pool scores across versions (old vs new never mixed)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC sub-bullet: 'Sidecars missing the `impact_model_version` tag are assigned a sentinel version label (e.g., `legacy-v1`) by the replay, never rejected or silently dropped; the replay logs a count of such records per run.'  In `calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py`, implement: `version = row.get('impact_model_version', 'legacy-v1')` before the segment-filter step.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the fallback behavior for sidecars missing `impact_model_version`: e.g., classify them as a named legacy bucket (e.g., `"pre-v2"`), skip them entirely, or emit a warning — and document this rule in the plan.
findings.finding: No fallback is specified for REVIEW_RESULT sidecars that lack the `impact_model_version` tag — i.e., sidecars written by old-code instances during the permissive rollout window or pre-existing corpus documents. The plan's 'never mix' invariant requires the replay to categorize every sidecar, but the handling of untagged records is undefined.
findings.location: What section / AC2
findings.suggested_fix: Treat absent `impact_model_version` as a sentinel value `"pre-v2"` (or `null`), log a count of untagged sidecars at script startup, and exclude them from any versioned comparison by default.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR (or CLAUDE.md / design-doc section) capturing the `impact_model_version` sidecar contract, the anti-pooling invariant, and the rationale for version-segmented replay, and reference it from the calibration script's docstring.
findings.finding: The `impact_model_version` sidecar field (a new cross-stage data contract) and the version-segmented-replay design (refusing to pool scores across model versions) are novel architectural decisions, but the plan names no ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or schema-doc destination to capture them.
findings.location: What / Scope / AC line 2
findings.suggested_fix: Create `docs/decisions/ADR-NNN-impact-model-versioning.md` covering: (1) the `impact_model_version` field definition and allowed values, (2) the anti-pooling invariant for the replay, (3) the rationale (corpus contamination risk). Reference this ADR in `calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py` and in any gate sidecar schema file.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `scripts/plan_review_impact_distribution.py` to the list of consumers that must filter by `impact_model_version` (or be replaced by the versioned calibration replay) during the rollout period.
findings.finding: `scripts/plan_review_impact_distribution.py` reads every REVIEW_RESULT sidecar to compute the impact-percentile distribution that feeds `novelty_priority_floor` config, but it is not listed as a consumer that needs updating when new fields (`impact_model_version`, new impact formula) are emitted. If the script pools findings across model versions it will produce a contaminated baseline during the permissive rollout period.
findings.location: Scope section / What section — named edit targets
findings.suggested_fix: Either extend `scripts/plan_review_impact_distribution.py` with the same `impact_model_version` filter the calibration script will gain, or document that it must not be run against a mixed-version corpus (and explain what to use instead).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the specific metric(s) and minimum delta for 'beating the current model' (e.g., 'AUC-ROC on the adjudicated set must exceed current model by ≥0.02') in AC4 and the A/B harness description.
findings.finding: 'Beating the current model' (AC4) is undefined — the metric (AUC-ROC, precision at fixed recall, F1, separation gap?) and the minimum improvement required to 'gate' a threshold-down change are both unspecified, leaving the gating criterion un-evaluable.
findings.location: AC4 / What section ('diff-grounded A/B harness')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reframe AC4 as a specific observable outcome: name the gating artifact and its enforcement mechanism (e.g., 'the A/B script exits non-zero and prints FAIL if the new model does not exceed the baseline metric, and threshold-config PRs require a passing script run as a CI check').
findings.finding: AC4's 'gates any threshold-down tuning' describes a mechanism/process rather than a specific observable outcome — it is effort-framed and does not state what artifact or system state changes when the gate is operative (a CI check? a config lock? a report file with a pass/fail field?).
findings.location: AC4

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify (or spec in the data-capture child) exactly which new fields the code-review sidecar (`code_review_result_v1`) must gain to satisfy AC3 — the current schema is missing `validity`, `impact`, `priority`, `tier`, `norm_id`, `material_fingerprint`, and `impact_model_version`.
findings.finding: The plan claims 'Both gates emit sidecars carrying the new impact + tier-tagged sub-answers + join keys' (AC3/What), but the code-review sidecar (code_review/sidecar.py, schema `code_review_result_v1`) currently lacks `validity`, `impact`, `priority`, `tier`, `norm_id`, and `material_fingerprint` fields — the capability assertion is contingent on the unshipped data-capture child, not a verified existing capability.
findings.location: AC3: 'Both gates emit sidecars carrying the new impact + tier-tagged sub-answers + join keys'; Scope: 'Depends on the two impact-redesign children and the data-capture child'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the recalibration trigger (e.g., 'after every 200 new adjudicated reviews, or monthly, whichever comes first') and the artifact location (e.g., 'in docs/experiments/calibration_cadence.md') in AC5.
findings.finding: 'Documented recalibration cadence... as the corpus grows' (AC5) defers the trigger definition to the executor — neither a corpus-size threshold nor a time interval is specified, so any written cadence satisfies the AC.
findings.location: AC5 / Scope bullet 4

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify deploy ordering for the three dependent children (impact-redesign x2, data-capture) and define the safe coexistence behavior when only a subset are deployed.
findings.finding: The plan depends on 'two impact-redesign children and the data-capture child' but does not specify deploy ordering or old+new coexistence semantics. If the data-capture child is deployed after the gates start emitting new-format sidecars, or vice versa, sidecars may be written in an inconsistent format during the window.
findings.location: Scope — 'Depends on the two impact-redesign children and the data-capture child'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a deploy order section: data-capture child first (schema-additive, backward compatible), then impact-redesign children, then this plan's permissive-threshold config flip. Specify that old gates writing sidecars without `impact_model_version` are treated as 'legacy' by the replay.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add observability for `impact_model_version` tagging: log/metric per gate emit showing the version tag value, and an alert if any sidecar is emitted without a recognized version tag.
findings.finding: No observability (metric, log, or alert) is specified for the new failure point: a gate emitting a sidecar without a valid `impact_model_version` tag. Operators have no signal that corpus contamination is occurring.
findings.location: What / Acceptance Criteria — sidecar tagging
findings.suggested_fix: Emit a structured log line and increment a counter metric (e.g., `review_sidecar_emitted{impact_model_version=...}`) at each gate emit; alert if `impact_model_version` is absent or equals an unexpected value.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Align AC4 to explicitly verify the A/B harness script (e.g., 'a diff-grounded A/B script/harness exists and produces a separation report…') rather than only the report output, so the reusable tooling is a verifiable deliverable.
findings.finding: The What section describes a 'diff-grounded A/B harness' (a script artifact) while AC4 verifies only a 'diff-grounded A/B report' (its output). The harness as a deliverable is never directly verified — only its output is tested. If the script is broken or absent, AC4 could still be satisfied by a manual report.
findings.location: What section ('diff-grounded A/B harness') vs AC4 ('diff-grounded A/B report')
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC4 to: 'A diff-grounded A/B harness (extending calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py) exists, scores the adjudicated set with the new model, reports separation of block-worthy vs nit, and gates any threshold-down tuning on beating the current model.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the concrete permissive threshold values (or the derivation rule, e.g. 'set both thresholds to the maximum of the scoring range so no review is hard-blocked') in the Scope/AC1 section.
findings.finding: 'Permissive thresholds' is not quantified — an executor cannot set the config values without guessing. The plan's only constraint is 'no new hard blocks at rollout', but the actual numeric values (or the rule for deriving them, e.g. 'set score threshold to 1.0 / the maximum permissive bound') are absent.
findings.location: Scope bullet 1 / AC1
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC1: 'Permissive threshold = [maximum score / 1.0 / equivalent no-block sentinel]; verified by asserting zero hard blocks in a corpus replay against the adjudicated set.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command or artifact-inspection step for each AC (e.g., for AC2: `python calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py --assert-no-version-pool` exits non-zero when mixed versions are supplied; for AC3: `jq -e '.impact_model_version' <sidecar.json>` returns a non-null string).
findings.finding: No proving command exists for any of the five ACs — every criterion is stated as a desired outcome ('records', 'filters', 'emits', 'measures', 'gates', 'documented') with no concrete verification command that would produce falsifiable evidence of completion.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria section (all five ACs)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that exercises the end-to-end live path: submit a synthetic review through each gate in the deployed environment and assert the resulting sidecar contains `impact_model_version`, new impact scores, and join keys (e.g., via `jq -e '.impact_model_version and .norm_id' <live_sidecar>`).
findings.finding: The new scoring path is shipped (permissive but active in production), yet no AC exercises the end-to-end live path — verifying that a real review actually produces a sidecar with valid new-model scores and the `impact_model_version` tag in the live environment, not only via offline replay of pre-existing artifacts.
findings.location: AC3 / What section ('Both gates emit sidecars')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan to the plan (e.g., 'revert impact_model config flags to restore old-model scoring; the old model remains live until threshold-down tuning is approved') so the reversibility mechanism is stated, not just implied.
findings.finding: The plan introduces new impact models that change scoring behavior for all in-flight and future reviews, but provides no explicit rollback or back-out plan if the models behave unexpectedly even under permissive thresholds. Config flags are implied as the rollback mechanism but never stated as such, violating T4's requirement that reversibility be explicitly specified — acknowledgement alone is insufficient.
findings.location: Scope section ('Config flags for permissive thresholds'); What section ('Ship both redesigned impact models behind PERMISSIVE thresholds')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' subsection to Scope: 'The config flags are also the rollback mechanism — setting them back to old-model values restores previous scoring behavior. The old impact model formula remains in the codebase until the A/B threshold-down gate is passed.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify error handling and idempotency for sidecar writes that include `impact_model_version`: define retry behavior on write failure, what constitutes a valid partial sidecar, and how the replay handles sidecars with an absent or malformed version tag.
findings.finding: The plan introduces sidecar writes with a new `impact_model_version` field but specifies no error handling, retry, or idempotency guarantee for these writes. A partial or failed sidecar write would silently corrupt the corpus the calibration replay depends on.
findings.location: What / Scope — sidecar emit; AC line 'Both gates emit sidecars carrying the new impact...'
findings.suggested_fix: Add: (1) atomic sidecar write (write-to-temp then rename); (2) the replay treats sidecars with missing `impact_model_version` as a distinct 'legacy/unknown' segment and logs a warning metric — never silently pools them with a named version.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a RETIRE path for the sidecar corpus: specify a retention window or archival trigger per `impact_model_version` so old-model sidecars don't accumulate unboundedly.
findings.finding: No retirement or cleanup path is specified for the accumulated sidecar corpus. Sidecars tagged with old model versions will accumulate indefinitely, with no defined point at which they are archived, pruned, or superseded.
findings.location: Scope — sidecar lifecycle; 'IMPACT-MODEL VERSIONING' section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a documented retention policy (e.g., 'sidecars from superseded model versions are archived to cold storage after the subsequent model version has accumulated N=500 adjudicated samples and calibration is complete').

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge known downstream consumers of REVIEW_RESULT sidecars and confirm the schema additions are backward-compatible (or specify a migration/versioning guard) so no consumer silently pools old-formula and new-formula scores during the rollout window.
findings.finding: The plan changes the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar schema (adds `impact_model_version`, `norm_id`, `material_fingerprint`, tier-tagged sub-answers) but does not acknowledge downstream consumers — such as the calibration replay, dashboards, or any other pipeline step — that currently read sidecars and may need to handle the new fields or version-segment logic.
findings.location: Scope section / AC line 3 ('Both gates emit sidecars carrying the new impact + tier-tagged sub-answers + join keys')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a short 'Backward compatibility' note in Scope: list known sidecar consumers (replay script, any dashboards), confirm all new fields are additive/optional, and state that the replay script extension (filtering by `impact_model_version`) ships atomically with or before the new model rollout to close the contamination window.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name `src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py` explicitly as an edit target (or confirm the data-capture child owns it) and list the exact fields it must add to `build_payload` so AC3 is verifiable at this ticket's boundary.
findings.finding: `src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py` is the concrete file that must be edited to carry `impact_model_version`, `norm_id`/`material_fingerprint` join keys, and tier-tagged sub-answers for code-review REVIEW_RESULT sidecars, but the plan never names it as an edit target — it delegates entirely to an unnamed 'data-capture child'.
findings.location: What section: 'Both gates emit sidecars … (code-review via the data-capture child)'; Scope section: 'Depends on … the data-capture child'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Justify `impact_model_version` as a distinct event-level field vs extending the existing finding-level `cohort` convention (e.g., explain why event-level vs finding-level granularity is needed, or confirm they serve different axes).
findings.finding: The plan proposes a new `impact_model_version` sidecar field for cross-version corpus segmentation, but the existing `cohort` tag in plan_review/sidecar.py already provides per-run/per-formula segmentation for exactly this purpose (WS9). The plan does not justify why a separate field is needed rather than extending the cohort value convention (e.g., `cohort: ['plan-v2', ...]`).
findings.location: What section: 'IMPACT-MODEL VERSIONING … every REVIEW_RESULT sidecar records an `impact_model_version` tag'; Scope section
findings.suggested_fix: If the version is uniform across all findings in a single review run, a top-level event field is correct — state this explicitly and note it complements rather than duplicates cohort (which can vary per finding within a run).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Assert post-rollback schema state: confirm old-code sidecar readers tolerate unknown fields (forward-compatibility), specify that `impact_model_version` is treated as an optional/additive field that is never stripped on rollback, and document this invariant in the Scope section.
findings.finding: The plan does not assert the schema state of already-written sidecars after a code rollback. If the new code is rolled back, sidecars written during the new-code window retain the `impact_model_version` field; the plan does not confirm that old-code readers are forward-compatible with the new field (tolerate unknown keys) or describe whether the field must be stripped/ignored post-rollback.
findings.location: Scope section / AC line 2 (impact_model_version sidecar field)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly state whether the 'labeled/adjudicated set' (impact-labeled block-worthy vs nit cases) must be CREATED as part of this story's scope, or cite the existing artifact path — and if it must be created, add it as a Scope item and AC so it's not silently assumed to exist.
findings.finding: The plan's A/B harness (AC4, What section) asserts it will 'score the labeled/adjudicated set' and 'measures separation of block-worthy vs nit on the adjudicated set,' implying an existing, impact-labeled corpus. The codebase has `IMPACT_EXPECTS = frozenset({'high_impact', 'low_impact'})` defined in `eval_scorers.py` but zero eval-spec dataset entries use those labels — both `plan-review-finder.eval.yaml` and `code-review-base.eval.yaml` explicitly state 'seed labels pending adjudication.' The adjudicated impact corpus the A/B harness depends on does not yet demonstrably exist.
findings.location: AC4 / What section ('diff-grounded A/B harness that scores the labeled/adjudicated set')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a Scope line: 'Seed the impact-adjudicated eval dataset: annotate N≥25 findings per gate (plan/code) with `expect: high_impact` / `expect: low_impact` labels as the A/B harness ground-truth corpus.' Then reference it from AC4: 'using the seeded impact-labeled eval dataset (see Scope).' If the corpus already exists outside the eval specs, cite its path explicitly.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
